workflow.name: Register users and authenticate with magic links using Google Sheets
workflow.description:
This workflow implements a lightweight authentication system for n8n web portals using Google Sheets as a simple user and session store. It supports email-based registration, username and password login, one-click magic link authentication, and cookie-based session management.

How it works
1. Users register with an email address through a form.
2. The workflow generates a password and a one-time authentication token.
3. User credentials and token are stored in Google Sheets.
4. An email is sent with login credentials and a magic link.
5. Users can log in with username and password or click the magic link.
6. The /auth endpoint validates the token, creates a session id (sid), sets it as an HttpOnly cookie, and invalidates the token.
7. The /profile endpoint reads the sid cookie, verifies the session in Google Sheets, and displays a protected profile page.

Set up steps
- Create a Google Sheet with columns: username, password, role, token, sid.
- Replace YOUR_SPREADSHEET_ID with your Google Sheet ID in all Google Sheets nodes.
- Replace YOUR_DOMAIN with your n8n instance domain in redirect URLs and email links.
- Configure Gmail or SMTP credentials in the email node.
- Ensure your n8n instance is served over HTTPS so Secure cookies function correctly.

This template is suitable for small internal portals, admin dashboards, or proof-of-concept authentication systems built entirely within n8n.

workflow.name: Register users and authenticate with magic links using Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## 1. Login
Validate username and password against Google Sheets, then reuse or issue a new token and redirect to the auth endpoint.

## 0. Register
Collect an email address via form, generate credentials and a one-time token, save the user to Google Sheets, and send an onboarding email with the login link.

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet

parameters.content:
## 2. Auth
Validate the one-time token via Google Sheets, generate a session id (sid), set an HttpOnly cookie, clear the token, and redirect to the profile page.

## 3. Profile
Read the sid cookie, look up the active session in Google Sheets, and render a profile page — or redirect to login if the session is invalid.

parameters.formDescription: Password will be sent to your email

parameters.subject: =Your login credentials

parameters.completionMessage:
=

✅ Please check email {{ $('Create password and token').item.json.email }} to get your login credentials

After that, you can login here:
https://YOUR_DOMAIN/form/login

nodes.name: Redirect to auth with new token
Redirect to profile and set cookies

parameters.content:
## Overview
This workflow provides a lightweight authentication flow for n8n web portals using Google Sheets as a simple user/session store and email delivery for onboarding. It supports register, username/password login, one-click magic link auth, and a cookie-based profile page.

### How it works
1. User registers with an email address via a form
2. n8n generates a username, password, and a one-time auth token
3. User data is stored in Google Sheets
4. n8n sends an email containing credentials + a one-click login link (`/auth?token=...`)
5. User can log in with username/password or click the magic link
6. `/auth` validates the token, creates a session id (sid), sets it as an HttpOnly cookie, and invalidates the token
7. `/profile` reads the sid cookie, validates the session in Google Sheets, and displays user info to confirm login

### Setup
- Configure Gmail credentials in the Send email node
- Create a Google Sheet with columns: username, password, role, token, sid
- Add your Google Sheets URL (replace YOUR_SPREADSHEET_ID)
- Replace YOUR_DOMAIN with your n8n instance domain in all nodes
- Ensure your n8n instance is served over HTTPS so Secure cookies work

### Customization
- Adjust token expiry (e.g., 10-15 minutes) and one-time use behavior
- Change session cookie settings (name, Max-Age, SameSite)
- Customize the registration email content
- Replace the Profile HTML with your own portal UI
- Add a /logout endpoint to revoke sessions
- Add rate-limiting at reverse proxy (Nginx/Traefik/Cloudflare)

main.node: Redirect to profile and set cookies
Redirect to auth with new token
Append or update row in sheet

user.name: ICTS Automation
user.bio:
ICTS Automation provides business process automation for SMEs, helping companies reduce manual work, minimize errors, and boost productivity. We design and deploy workflow automation solutions using tools like n8n, Microsoft Power Automate, and custom AI bots that fit your existing systems.

Our goal is to help small and medium businesses simplify operations, save time, and focus on growth instead of repetitive tasks.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
